# Figure description: Страт_колонки_скважин_черт_49_л_7.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Страт_колонки_совмещенные_по_скважинам/Страт_колонки_скважин_черт_49_л_7.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **composite stratigraphic/lithological column diagram** (геологический разрез / литолого-стратиграфические колонки) — specifically a set of **comparative borehole lithological-stratigraphic sections** showing multiple drillhole logs correlated side by side. It is a Soviet-style "сводная колонка" (composite/summary column) or correlation panel, with individual borehole columns displayed at the same vertical scale and connected by correlation lines.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure shows **multiple borehole (скважина) lithological columns** from the **Чернобыльского (Chernobilsky) or similar named horizon/area** in northern Kazakhstan, correlated across several wells. Based on the titles visible at the tops of individual columns, the boreholes appear to include references to:

- **Скважины (boreholes)** numbered in ranges visible as: **Т-20, 25, 27, 371, 368, 318, 320, 321** and others
- References to **скв-629–802** (or similar range) for at least one column on the right side
- Several columns are labeled as **геологический разрез по скважинам берега Чернобыльского горизонта** (geological section along boreholes of the Chernobilsky horizon/shore)
- Depth ranges appear to extend from surface to approximately **400–500 m** based on the column lengths and depth markers visible

The stratigraphic units labeled on the columns include Cyrillic formation/horizon codes such as:
- **C₁d** (Devonian Carboniferous boundary units)
- **C₁t**, **C₁v**, **C₂b**, **C₂m**, **C₃** (various Carboniferous subdivisions)
- **P** (Permian)
- **K** (Cretaceous, visible as a large letter on one column)
- **N** (Neogene, also visible as a large letter)
- Depth markers in **meters** are noted alongside each column

---

## Key Content

The figure presents **six to eight individual vertical lithological columns** arranged left to right, with **correlation lines** drawn between equivalent stratigraphic horizons across boreholes. Key observations:

1. **Lithological patterns** within the columns show a variety of rock types indicated by standard Soviet geological hatching symbols:
   - **Dotted/stippled patterns** — sandstones or sandy sediments
   - **Brick/block patterns** — limestones or carbonates
   - **Horizontal line patterns** — clays or shales
   - **Diagonal cross-hatch** — possibly dolomites or crystalline/altered rocks
   - **Dense stipple** — possibly siltstones or tuffs

2. **Correlation lines** connect equivalent beds/horizons between adjacent columns, demonstrating lateral continuity or discontinuity of key stratigraphic markers across the section. Some correlation lines show **pronounced dip or offset**, suggesting structural variation (folding or faulting) between borehole locations.

3. The **vertical scale** appears uniform across all columns (likely **1:500 or 1:1000** based on column length vs. depth annotations), allowing direct visual comparison of bed thicknesses.

4. The **leftmost column** is the longest/deepest and appears to be the **reference or key borehole**, with the most complete stratigraphic record.

5. **Формационные индексы** (formation indices) are marked at regular intervals on the right side of each column (e.g., C₁d, C₂b, P₁, etc.), identifying the stratigraphic age of each interval.

6. The **lower portions** of several columns show **darker/denser hatching** consistent with older Paleozoic basement or carbonate sequences.

7. There is a **stamp/seal** (гербовая печать) in the lower right area — a Soviet institutional library or archive stamp — reading something akin to **"Библиотека СССР... геологическая фонды... инв. №30222"** (Library of the USSR... geological funds... inventory No. 30222).

---
